content.text:
Memory stage: NoteReconciler v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate notes should update active note memory.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_note_id": "existing-note-id-or-null",
              "patch": null,
              "links_to_create": [
                {
                  "to_note_id": "existing-note-id",
                  "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
                  "link_weight": 1.0
                }
              ],
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new reusable context.
        - noop: candidate duplicates an existing active note or is lower value.
        - supersede: candidate is a better current version of target_note_id.
        - update: patch target_note_id without inserting a new note.
        - retract: target_note_id should no longer be active because TARGET_SOURCE retracts it.

        Rules:
        - Compare semantic meaning, not wording.
        - Do not keep duplicate notes just because titles differ.
        - Prefer updating or superseding an existing note when the candidate refines the same decision, rationale, plan, or procedure.
        - Insert when the candidate captures a distinct reusable semantic fragment.
        - Use noop for summary spam, weak paraphrases, or information already captured by an existing note.
        - Use links_to_create only for strong semantic relations between notes.
        - target_note_id must be one of Existing active notes below or null.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate notes below.
        - Return one operation per candidate unless TARGET_SOURCE only retracts or updates an existing note.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB
Document lines: 547-558

## 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB

`Entity + Claim + NoteLink + scope_json` уже дают graph-like semantics.
Для memory layer обычно хватает:

- Postgres
- JSONB
- pgvector
- full-text indexes
- lineage tables

Graph DB нужен, когда traversal/path queries становятся частью продукта, а не просто внутренним удобством.

        Candidate notes:
        [0] type=DIRECTION title=Start memory layer without Graph DB by default summary=The architecture recommends not starting with a Graph DB because graph-like semantics are already provided by `Entity + Claim + NoteLink + scope_json`, and a Postgres-based stack is usually sufficient for the memory layer. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md entities=entity:<0>,entity:<1> keywords=Graph DB,Entity + Claim + NoteLink + scope_json,memory layer tags=architecture,storage_choice,graph_db evidence=`Entity + Claim + NoteLink + scope_json` уже дают graph-like semantics.
[1] type=DOC_DIGEST title=Sufficient default storage stack for memory layer summary=For the memory layer, the document lists a sufficient default stack: Postgres, JSONB, pgvector, full-text indexes, and lineage tables. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md entities=entity:<2>,entity:<3>,entity:<4>,entity:<5>,entity:<6>,entity:<1> keywords=Postgres,JSONB,pgvector,full-text indexes,lineage tables tags=architecture,default_stack,memory_layer evidence=Для memory layer обычно хватает: - Postgres - JSONB - pgvector - full-text indexes - lineage tables

        Existing active notes:
        id=hot-path:note:<0> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Read path policy maps query types to retrieval modes and capped budgets summary=Section 12 specifies when memory retrieval is needed, how to choose mode (claim-first, note-first, mixed, evidence-first/fallback), and explicit v3 budget caps per mode; answer composition must distinguish verified vs provisional, handle conflicts, honor scope, abstain when needed, and avoid presenting notes as hard facts. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 12. Read path' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=Read path,retrieval mode,budget caps v3,verified vs provisional tags=retrieval-policy,answer-composition,memory-architecture useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<1> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Storage tiers define a five-layer memory architecture with distinct roles summary=The architecture separates memory into Tier A (core prompt context), Tier B (typed long-term records), Tier C (rich semantic notes), Tier D (raw evidence), and Tier E (retrieval indexes), with each tier explicitly defining what should and should not be stored there. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 5. Storage tiers' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=Storage tiers,Tier A,Tier B,Tier C,Tier D,Tier E tags=memory_architecture,tiered_storage,doc_digest useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<2> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Responsibility split across runtime, memory worker, and storage layer summary=Section 10 assigns clear boundaries: User-facing runtime handles dialog, tool orchestration, read-time retrieval, and cheap routing/escalation; Memory worker/manager owns routing, retrieval-before-update, canonicalization, extraction, reconciliation, consolidation, repair, and retention; Storage layer provides immutable evidence, typed tables, transactional updates, lineage, indexes, and audit tra...[truncated 3 chars] scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 10. Разделение ответственности' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=User-facing runtime,Memory worker / manager,Storage layer,separation of responsibilities tags=component_roles,architecture,operating_model useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<3> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Claim vs Note write policy in memory architecture summary=The section defines a decision rule: write Claim for explicit, normalizable, precision-answerable information comparable by predicate policy; write Note for important retrieval-relevant context that is too rich or too early for hard facts; write both when material contains explicit facts/tasks plus rationale or direction. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 7. Когда писать `Claim`, а когда `Note`' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=Claim vs Note,predicate policy,write policy tags=memory_architecture,ingest_policy,claim_note_split useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<4> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Recommended flow routes all write modes through Write-Time Retrieval and Entity Canonicalizer before specialized updaters summary=The recommended architecture routes noop/direct_structured_write/note_write/mixed decisions from Memory Router, and all non-noop writes pass through Write-Time Retrieval then Entity Canonicalizer before Claim Extractor, Task Updater, Profile Updater, and Note Constructor, with reconciliation and background workers around shared storage. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 3. Основная модель записи .. 4. Рекомендуемая архитектура' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=Memory Router,Write-Time Retrieval,Entity Canonicalizer,Read-Time Retrieval Planner tags=architecture_flow,pipeline,component_roles useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<5> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Optional fast/slow execution split with typed handoff objects summary=The architecture proposes an optional next-step split: a fast path for user-facing low-latency draft/escalation-gate flow, and a slow path for stronger processing (conflict resolution, answer verification, note consolidation, memory repair). Handoffs between paths should use typed objects rather than free text. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 16. Optional fast/slow execution path' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=fast path,slow path,typed handoff objects,escalation gate,memory repair tags=execution_architecture,latency,handoff_contract useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<6> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Memory architecture v3 is organized around four axes summary=The architecture defines memory as a 4-axis system (Representation, Lifecycle, Retrieval policy, Scope and lineage) to avoid degenerate designs like transcript dumps or brittle fact stores. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 1. Цель системы .. 2. Центральная архитектурная идея' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=Memory architecture v3,four axes,Representation,Lifecycle,Retrieval policy,Scope and lineage tags=architecture,memory_system,design useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<7> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Truth state and Retrieval state are orthogonal status dimensions summary=The architecture treats Truth state and Retrieval state as separate dimensions: Truth state tracks knowledge validity per record type (different enums for claims and notes), while Retrieval state tracks hot-path/search handling (`active`, `warm`, `cold`, `archived`, `deleted`). A record can combine both independently (e.g., `resolved`+`warm`, `superseded`+`warm`). scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 8. Truth state и retrieval state' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=Truth state,Retrieval state,active warm cold archived deleted tags=state_model,lifecycle,retrieval_policy useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<8> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Write path v3 defines an 8-step transactional memory update pipeline summary=Section 11 defines a strict write pipeline: persist source, route mode, retrieve candidate memories before extraction, canonicalize entities, run typed extractors, reconcile with existing items, apply all updates in one transaction, then schedule maintenance. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 11. Write path' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=Write path,Apply transaction,Reconcile,Schedule maintenance tags=memory-architecture,write-pipeline,transactional-update useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<9> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Memory updates must not be append-only and require retrieval/reconciliation summary=The architecture defines a mandatory write pipeline: decide whether to write, find similar/conflicting items, convert input into typed candidates, and apply a policy (`insert`, `update`, `supersede`, `retract`, `noop`). Skipping retrieval/reconciliation causes duplicates, stale preferences, conflicting notes, task explosion, and summary spam. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 9. Главная мысль: update не должен быть append-only' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=append-only update,retrieval-before-update,reconciliation,insert update supersede retract noop tags=memory_architecture,write_policy,deduplication useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<10> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Note maturity lifecycle and promotion/demotion criteria summary=Section 13 defines a note lifecycle to prevent `Note` from becoming a summary store: `fresh` → `stabilizing` → `mature` → `consolidated`. Maturity increases with retrieval use, multi-source confirmation, conflict-free survival over time, and recurring rationale relevance; it decreases or closes with explicit supersede, stale scope, non-use, or weak evidence. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 13. Note maturity' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=Note maturity,fresh,stabilizing,mature,consolidated tags=memory_architecture,note_lifecycle,retrieval_quality useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<11> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=AI agent memory layer goals emphasize durable, updateable, and traceable memory summary=The system goal is a memory layer that persists across sessions, stores multiple knowledge types (facts, preferences, tasks, rationale, plans), supports knowledge updates, preserves provenance, avoids writing every turn permanently, and includes aging/archive/repair behaviors. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 1. Цель системы .. 2. Центральная архитектурная идея' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=AI agent memory layer,system goals,provenance,knowledge update,archive repair tags=requirements,memory_policy,architecture_goals useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<12> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Memory repair is a separate background workflow from consolidation summary=Section 14 states that Memory repair is a distinct background workflow focused on memory health issues (duplicates, contradictions, profile drift, stale tasks, bad merges, note spam, retrieval budget leakage). It is explicitly separated from consolidation: consolidation makes memory richer, while repair makes memory healthier. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 14. Memory repair' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=Memory repair,consolidation,background workflow,memory health tags=maintenance,repair_workflow,consolidation useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<13> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=v3 retention/forgetting is policy-driven with explicit actions and triggers summary=In memory architecture v3, forgetting is a formal policy rather than passive decay. The section defines minimum forgetting actions (downgrade retrieval state, archive, soft delete, hard delete by request) and minimum triggers (namespace policy, age, low use_count, legal/privacy request, explicit user forget/delete command). scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 15. Retention / forgetting' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=v3 forgetting policy,retention,soft delete,hard delete,use_count tags=memory_architecture,retention_policy,forgetting useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<14> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Structured scope v3 requires dual scope representation with mandatory scope_json fields summary=In v3, scope must be stored both as human-readable `scope_text` and machine-readable `scope_json`; at minimum `scope_json` includes `scope_kind`, `project_id`, `environment`, `subject_entity_id`, `conversation_id`, `modality`, and `audience` to improve reconciliation and reduce retrieval overgeneralization. scope=Applies to section '01. Architecture / 6. Structured scope' in agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md keywords=Structured scope,v3,scope_text,scope_json,scope_kind,project_id tags=scope_model,retrieval_quality,reconciliation,architecture_direction useCount=0

responseFormat.description: Reconcile note candidates against active memory notes.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_reconciler

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

responseFormat.name: memory_note_reconciler
responseFormat.description: Reconcile note candidates against active memory notes.

structured.fullText: {"operations":[{"action":"insert","candidate_index":0,"links_to_create":[],"patch":null,"reason":"New architecture direction from section 17: do not start with Graph DB by default for memory layer; this semantic is not captured in existing active notes.","target_note_id":null},{"action":"insert","candidate_index":1,"links_to_create":[{"link_type":"refines","link_weight":0.74,"to_note_id":"hot-path:note:<1>"}],"patch":null,"reason":"Distinct reusable detail listing the sufficient default storage stack (Postgres, JSONB, pgvector, full-text, lineage); complements and refines existing storage-tier digest without duplicating it.","target_note_id":null}]}
